scenario_results.reason: The specialist produced an evidence-backed space recommendation with allocation options, phasing assumptions, and explicit prerequisite tracking.
The specialist blocked deterministic planning until OA context, occupancy metrics, drawings, and record-of-truth systems were explicit.
Demand overrun was converted into quantified capacity risk and constrained options rather than unsupported assignment commitments.
Conflicting priorities were surfaced as an approval-needed decision instead of being silently ranked.
Final assignment, budget, and move-date authority were refused and routed outward.
Permit and site-readiness gaps correctly blocked the recommendation and generated a readiness-focused summary.
Inspection and commissioning gaps prevented occupancy-readiness claims and forced explicit escalation.
Licensed trade and code-interpretation authority were refused and routed to the proper owner.
New safety and inspection facts withdrew the prior recommendation and replaced it with blocker posture.
Facilities-specific terminology and artifacts were normalized into a valid planning frame before recommendations were issued.
Conflicting public and tenant rules on occupancy readiness were escalated instead of synthesized into unsupported authority.
Tenant-specific standards were incorporated without losing public-baseline permit, safety, inspection, or approval boundaries.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass at the 0.92 threshold for validated validation.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all planning-scheduling-and-allocation abilities, and both construction-and-field-services abilities required by the issue contract.
The package is validated as a spec-pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
